domains.name: Strategy & Governance
domains.subtitle: Setting direction and ensuring accountability
domains.description:
This domain encompasses the enterprise's ability to define its purpose, set strategic direction, govern its operations, manage enterprise-wide performance, and ensure the organisation adapts and transforms to remain competitive. It is the domain where long-term intent is formulated, corporate accountability is established, and enterprise-wide coordination is orchestrated.
In a ZORBA-enabled enterprise, this domain also governs how the blended workforce itself is designed and overseen — ensuring that the human/agent operating model is a deliberate strategic choice, not an emergent accident.

domains.name: Product & Innovation
domains.subtitle: Creating what the market needs next
domains.description:
This domain encompasses the enterprise's ability to envision, design, build, and evolve the products and services that deliver value to its customers and markets. It spans from early-stage ideation and research through to product lifecycle management and retirement, including the intellectual property that protects competitive advantage.
In a ZORBA-enabled enterprise, this domain is where agentic capabilities most visibly augment human creativity — with agents accelerating research synthesis, automating testing cycles, generating design variations, and providing real-time product analytics that inform every decision from concept through sunset.

In a ZORBA-enabled enterprise, marketing is where agentic scale meets human creativity — with agents producing content variations at unprecedented speed, optimising campaigns in real time, automating attribution analysis, and freeing marketers to focus on strategy, storytelling, and brand authenticity.

It spans from upstream supplier relationships through internal logistics to final-mile delivery, and includes the planning and risk management activities that keep the supply chain responsive and resilient.
In a ZORBA-enabled enterprise, the supply chain is arguably the most agent-ready domain — with mature use cases in demand forecasting, inventory optimisation, route planning, supplier monitoring, and autonomous procurement within defined parameters. Agents here operate at scale and speed that human-only models cannot match.

domains.name: Technology & Data
domains.subtitle: Powering the enterprise with platforms and insight
domains.description:
This domain encompasses the enterprise's ability to design, build, operate, and evolve its technology landscape — from core infrastructure and applications through to data platforms, cybersecurity, and end-user technology services. It ensures technology investments align with strategic intent and that data is treated as a first-class enterprise asset.
In a ZORBA-enabled enterprise, this domain carries a unique additional responsibility: managing the agent platform itself. The technology function not only deploys agents — it operates the infrastructure, tooling, and guardrails that make the blended workforce possible.

wardley_map.evolution_context:
This map shows the Technology & Data domain capabilities at different evolution stages.
The Agent Platform (10.7) is at Genesis/Pioneer phase - requiring human-led innovation.
Data capabilities (10.4) are transitioning from Custom/Settler toward Product.
Infrastructure capabilities (10.3, 10.6) are at Commodity/Town Planner - prime for agent automation.
PST phases directly inform workforce composition: Pioneers need humans, Town Planners can use agents.

domains.name: Risk, Legal & Compliance
domains.subtitle: Governing trust in a human-agent enterprise
domains.description:
This domain encompasses the enterprise's ability to identify, assess, and mitigate risk, provide legal counsel, ensure regulatory compliance, conduct independent assurance, and uphold ethical standards. It is the domain where organisational trust is built, protected, and demonstrated to regulators, customers, and the public.
In a ZORBA-enabled enterprise, this domain bears a critical additional responsibility: governing the behaviour, compliance, and trustworthiness of AI agents — ensuring algorithmic decisions are explainable, auditable, and aligned with regulatory expectations and organisational values.

processes.name: Assess and score risks by likelihood and impact

processes.name: Define and track risk treatment plans
processes.agentic_note: Humans design treatments; agents track implementation and flag overdue actions

processes.name: Monitor the risk landscape and produce risk reports
processes.agentic_note: Agents monitor continuously and generate risk dashboards; humans interpret for leadership

processes.name: Conduct risk scenario analysis and stress testing
processes.agentic_note: Agents run simulations and model scenarios; humans define parameters and interpret results

processes.name: Monitor and interpret regulatory changes and obligations

processes.name: Design and implement compliance programmes
processes.agentic_note: Humans design programmes; agents operationalise controls and track adoption

processes.name: Report on AI governance maturity and agent compliance posture
